revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:犯土]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11571306]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=June 2021}} '''Hando''' (犯土) is one of the [[selection days]] ({{ill|selection day|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/selection day|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}). Also referred to as '''Tsuchi''', '''Shii''', or '''Tsuchi'''.
From [[Kanoe-no-Uma]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Uma|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Uma|qq=}}, the seventh day from [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) to [[Hinoe-no-Ne]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ne|ja|1=Yang Fire Rat|2=ja|丙子|1=Yang Fire Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ne|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the thirteenth day), the seven days are called '''Dai-Hando''' (大犯土, "Great Hando" or "Great Earth"). From [[Tsuchinoe-no-Tora]] ({{ill|Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|ja|1=Yang Earth Tiger|2=ja|戊寅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fifteenth day) to [[Kanoe-no-Saru]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Saru|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Saru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the twenty-first day), the seven days are called '''Sho-Hando''' (小犯土, "Small Hando" or "Small Earth"). The intervening day, [[Hinoe-no-Ushi]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja|1=Yin Fire Ox|2=ja|丁丑|1=Yin Fire Ox|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fourteenth day), is referred to as '''Kanbi''' (間日, "Intercalary Day"), '''Hando Kanbi''' (犯土間日, "Intercalary Hando Day"), or '''Chu-Hando''' (中犯土, "Middle Hando"). This day is excluded from the Hando period or, alternatively, included but without the taboos associated with Hando.
During the Hando periods, it is believed that the {{ill|Tsuchikami|ja|土公神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchikami|qq=|1=Tsuchikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423100}} (Earth God) resides in their main shrine or within the earth, and thus the earth must not be disturbed. Activities such as digging, well-digging, sowing seeds, civil engineering works, and tree felling are all prohibited. Particularly for [[Jichinsai]] ([[:en:Jichinsai|Jichinsai]], ground-breaking ceremonies), Hando is considered an inauspicious time. These beliefs align closely with those associated with {{ill|Doyo|ja|土用|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyo|qq=|1=Doyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423778}}.

The intervening day, [[Hinoe-no-Ushi]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja|1=Yin Fire Ox|2=ja|丁丑|1=Yin Fire Ox|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fourteenth day), is referred to as '''Kanbi''' (間日, "Intercalary Day"), '''Hando Kanbi''' (犯土間日, "Intercalary Hando Day"), or '''Chu-Hando''' (中犯土, "Middle Hando"). This day is excluded from the Hando period or, alternatively, included but without the taboos associated with Hando.
During the Hando periods, it is believed that the {{ill|Tsuchikami|ja|土公神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchikami|1=Tsuchikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423100}} (Earth God) resides in their main shrine or within the earth, and thus the earth must not be disturbed. Activities such as digging, well-digging, sowing seeds, civil engineering works, and tree felling are all prohibited. Particularly for [[Jichinsai]] ([[:en:Jichinsai|Jichinsai]], ground-breaking ceremonies), Hando is considered an inauspicious time. These beliefs align closely with those associated with {{ill|Doyo|ja|土用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyo|1=Doyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423778}}.
Originally, Hando referred to acts of "disturbing the earth," particularly rituals corresponding to modern-day Jichinsai. Over time, the term evolved to encompass the prohibitions and periods associated with these acts.

Also referred to as '''Tsuchi''', '''Shii''', or '''Tsuchi'''.
From [[Kanoe-no-Uma]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Uma|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Uma|qq=}}, the seventh day from [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) to [[Hinoe-no-Ne]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ne|ja|1=Yang Fire Rat|2=ja|丙子|1=Yang Fire Rat|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ne|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the thirteenth day), the seven days are called '''Dai-Hando''' (大犯土, "Great Hando" or "Great Earth"). From [[Tsuchinoe-no-Tora]] ({{ill|Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|ja|1=Yang Earth Tiger|2=ja|戊寅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fifteenth day) to [[Kanoe-no-Saru]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Saru|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Saru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the twenty-first day), the seven days are called '''Sho-Hando''' (小犯土, "Small Hando" or "Small Earth"). The intervening day, [[Hinoe-no-Ushi]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja|1=Yin Fire Ox|2=ja|丁丑|1=Yin Fire Ox|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fourteenth day), is referred to as '''Kanbi''' (間日, "Intercalary Day"), '''Hando Kanbi''' (犯土間日, "Intercalary Hando Day"), or '''Chu-Hando''' (中犯土, "Middle Hando"). This day is excluded from the Hando period or, alternatively, included but without the taboos associated with Hando.
During the Hando periods, it is believed that the {{ill|Tsuchikami|ja|土公神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchikami|1=Tsuchikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423100}} (Earth God) resides in their main shrine or within the earth, and thus the earth must not be disturbed. Activities such as digging, well-digging, sowing seeds, civil engineering works, and tree felling are all prohibited. Particularly for [[Jichinsai]] ([[:en:Jichinsai|Jichinsai]], ground-breaking ceremonies), Hando is considered an inauspicious time. These beliefs align closely with those associated with {{ill|Doyo|ja|土用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyo|1=Doyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423778}}.
Originally, Hando referred to acts of "disturbing the earth," particularly rituals corresponding to modern-day Jichinsai. Over time, the term evolved to encompass the prohibitions and periods associated with these acts.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:犯土]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:犯土]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:犯土]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11571306]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=June 2021}} '''Hando''' (犯土) is one of the [[selection days]] ({{ill|selection day|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/selection day|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}). Also referred to as '''Tsuchi''', '''Shii''', or '''Tsuchi'''.
From [[Kanoe-no-Uma]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Uma|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Uma|qq=}}, the seventh day from [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) to [[Hinoe-no-Ne]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ne|ja|1=Yang Fire Rat|2=ja|丙子|1=Yang Fire Rat|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ne|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the thirteenth day), the seven days are called '''Dai-Hando''' (大犯土, "Great Hando" or "Great Earth"). From [[Tsuchinoe-no-Tora]] ({{ill|Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|ja|1=Yang Earth Tiger|2=ja|戊寅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fifteenth day) to [[Kanoe-no-Saru]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Saru|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Saru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the twenty-first day), the seven days are called '''Sho-Hando''' (小犯土, "Small Hando" or "Small Earth"). The intervening day, [[Hinoe-no-Ushi]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja|1=Yin Fire Ox|2=ja|丁丑|1=Yin Fire Ox|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fourteenth day), is referred to as '''Kanbi''' (間日, "Intercalary Day"), '''Hando Kanbi''' (犯土間日, "Intercalary Hando Day"), or '''Chu-Hando''' (中犯土, "Middle Hando"). This day is excluded from the Hando period or, alternatively, included but without the taboos associated with Hando.
During the Hando periods, it is believed that the {{ill|Tsuchikami|ja|土公神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchikami|1=Tsuchikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423100}} (Earth God) resides in their main shrine or within the earth, and thus the earth must not be disturbed. Activities such as digging, well-digging, sowing seeds, civil engineering works, and tree felling are all prohibited. Particularly for [[Jichinsai]] ([[:en:Jichinsai|Jichinsai]], ground-breaking ceremonies), Hando is considered an inauspicious time. These beliefs align closely with those associated with {{ill|Doyo|ja|土用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyo|1=Doyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423778}}.
Originally, Hando referred to acts of "disturbing the earth," particularly rituals corresponding to modern-day Jichinsai. Over time, the term evolved to encompass the prohibitions and periods associated with these acts.
==References== *{{Cite book|lang=ja |author1=岡田芳朗|authorlink1=岡田芳朗|author2=阿久根末忠|authorlink2=阿久根末忠 |year=1993 |title=現代こよみ読み解き事典 (Modern Almanac Interpretation Dictionary) |publisher=柏書房 (Kashiwa Shobō) |isbn=4-7601-0951-X }}
{{translated page|ja|犯土|version=89836938|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:犯土]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:犯土]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:犯土]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11571306]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=June 2021}} '''Hando''' (犯土) is one of the [[selection days]] ({{ill|selection day|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/selection day|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}). Also referred to as '''Tsuchi''', '''Shii''', or '''Tsuchi'''.
From [[Kanoe-no-Uma]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Uma|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Uma|qq=}}, the seventh day from {{ill|Wood Rat|en|Wood Rat|WD=Q1190890|lt=Yang Wood Rat}}) to [[Hinoe-no-Ne]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ne|ja|1=Yang Fire Rat|2=ja|丙子|1=Yang Fire Rat|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ne|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the thirteenth day), the seven days are called '''Dai-Hando''' (大犯土, "Great Hando" or "Great Earth"). From [[Tsuchinoe-no-Tora]] ({{ill|Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|ja|1=Yang Earth Tiger|2=ja|戊寅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fifteenth day) to [[Kanoe-no-Saru]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Saru|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Saru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the twenty-first day), the seven days are called '''Sho-Hando''' (小犯土, "Small Hando" or "Small Earth"). The intervening day, [[Hinoe-no-Ushi]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja|1=Yin Fire Ox|2=ja|丁丑|1=Yin Fire Ox|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fourteenth day), is referred to as '''Kanbi''' (間日, "Intercalary Day"), '''Hando Kanbi''' (犯土間日, "Intercalary Hando Day"), or '''Chu-Hando''' (中犯土, "Middle Hando"). This day is excluded from the Hando period or, alternatively, included but without the taboos associated with Hando.
During the Hando periods, it is believed that the {{ill|Tsuchikami|ja|土公神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchikami|1=Tsuchikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423100}} (Earth God) resides in their main shrine or within the earth, and thus the earth must not be disturbed. Activities such as digging, well-digging, sowing seeds, civil engineering works, and tree felling are all prohibited. Particularly for [[Jichinsai]] ({{ill|Jichinsai|en|Jichinsai|WD=Q15467069|lt=Jichinsai}}, ground-breaking ceremonies), Hando is considered an inauspicious time. These beliefs align closely with those associated with {{ill|Doyo|ja|土用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyo|1=Doyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423778}}.
Originally, Hando referred to acts of "disturbing the earth," particularly rituals corresponding to modern-day Jichinsai. Over time, the term evolved to encompass the prohibitions and periods associated with these acts.
==References== *{{Cite book|lang=ja |author1=岡田芳朗|authorlink1=岡田芳朗|author2=阿久根末忠|authorlink2=阿久根末忠 |year=1993 |title=現代こよみ読み解き事典 (Modern Almanac Interpretation Dictionary) |publisher=柏書房 (Kashiwa Shobō) |isbn=4-7601-0951-X }}

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=June 2021}} '''Hando''' (犯土) is one of the [[selection days]] ({{ill|selection day|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/selection day|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}). Also referred to as '''Tsuchi''', '''Shii''', or '''Tsuchi'''.
From [[Kanoe-no-Uma]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Uma|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Uma|qq=}}, the seventh day from {{ill|Wood Rat|en|Wood Rat|WD=Q1190890|lt=Yang Wood Rat|1=Yang Wood Rat}}) to [[Hinoe-no-Ne]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ne|ja|1=Yang Fire Rat|2=ja|丙子|1=Yang Fire Rat|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ne|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the thirteenth day), the seven days are called '''Dai-Hando''' (大犯土, "Great Hando" or "Great Earth"). From [[Tsuchinoe-no-Tora]] ({{ill|Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|ja|1=Yang Earth Tiger|2=ja|戊寅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-no-Tora|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fifteenth day) to [[Kanoe-no-Saru]] ({{ill|Kanoe-no-Saru|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe-no-Saru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the twenty-first day), the seven days are called '''Sho-Hando''' (小犯土, "Small Hando" or "Small Earth"). The intervening day, [[Hinoe-no-Ushi]] ({{ill|Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja|1=Yin Fire Ox|2=ja|丁丑|1=Yin Fire Ox|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe-no-Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the fourteenth day), is referred to as '''Kanbi''' (間日, "Intercalary Day"), '''Hando Kanbi''' (犯土間日, "Intercalary Hando Day"), or '''Chu-Hando''' (中犯土, "Middle Hando"). This day is excluded from the Hando period or, alternatively, included but without the taboos associated with Hando.
During the Hando periods, it is believed that the {{ill|Tsuchikami|ja|土公神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchikami|1=Q11423100|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423100}} (Earth God) resides in their main shrine or within the earth, and thus the earth must not be disturbed. Activities such as digging, well-digging, sowing seeds, civil engineering works, and tree felling are all prohibited. Particularly for [[Jichinsai]] ({{ill|Jichinsai|en|Jichinsai|WD=Q15467069|lt=Jichinsai|1=Jichinsai}}, ground-breaking ceremonies), Hando is considered an inauspicious time. These beliefs align closely with those associated with {{ill|Doyo|ja|土用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyo|1=Doyō (season)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423778}}.
Originally, Hando referred to acts of "disturbing the earth," particularly rituals corresponding to modern-day Jichinsai. Over time, the term evolved to encompass the prohibitions and periods associated with these acts.
